assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16.
The intragroup dividend has resulted in a recognized intra-group receivable and related foreign exchange exposure in the consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for changes in fair value of a recognized asset or liability attributable to a particular risk that could affect profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognized items or highly probable forecast transactions, including qualifying intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
